mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Excessively high inflation (CPIAUCSL > 220) triggers expectations of monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS > 100k) and declining unemployment (UNRATE < 5%) signals a tightening labor market, boosting corporate earnings and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The joint effect of PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to explain more than 10% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST > 600k) indicate robust demand for goods and services, which correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as firms scale production.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns show no significant correlation with HOUST in 3-year rolling windows (R-squared < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO > 93) reflects expanding supply capacity, which reduces input costs and improves corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to explain > 5% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Weak housing starts (HOUST < 550k) signal deteriorating demand for construction, which correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days due to sector-specific risk spillovers.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of instances where HOUST falls below 550.

mechanism_narrative: High consumer credit growth (RSAFS > 350k) combined with low interest rates (FEDFUNDS < 1%) indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting consumption, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The interaction between RSAFS and FEDFUNDS explains < 15% of SPX return variance in 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment (UNRATE < 4.5%) during a recovery phase signals tight labor markets, which drives wage inflation and corporate profit growth, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant positive correlation with UNRATE in 3-year rolling windows (R-squared < 0.05).
